title: Quick-commerce assortment under pick-slot limits
prompt: A neighborhood dark store chooses which products to make orderable for the next shift. Shelf and pick-slot capacity are fixed, demand is uncertain, and unavailable products may redirect demand to displayed substitutes. Frame the research problem without naming a real firm or claiming measured customer effects.

decision_variables: binary product inclusion for the shift
model_specification.mechanism: The offered set changes purchase probabilities and substitution, while included products consume scarce operating capacity.

fidelity_requirements: The objective is contribution margin, not social welfare
prohibited_inferences: Faster delivery is caused by assortment reduction

evidence_needs.claim: This policy raises realized profit at a named firm
